task_completion.description: Create a 'Waste flow' named 'Post-consumer HDPE packaging' with Mass (kg) as the reference property.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the OpenLCA GUI, a flow editor tab is open for 'Post-consumer HDPE packaging', showing the flow type as 'Waste flow' and the reference flow property as 'Mass' (kg).

task_completion.description: Connect the waste flow to a USLCI landfill or waste treatment process.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the OpenLCA GUI, a process or product system editor shows the 'Post-consumer HDPE packaging' flow listed as an input or output, connected to a landfill or waste treatment process originating from the USLCI database.

task_completion.description: Calculate LCIA results using the TRACI 2.1 method.
task_completion.visual_evidence: An OpenLCA results window or calculation setup screen is visible, explicitly showing that the 'TRACI 2.1' impact assessment method was selected and used for the calculation.

task_completion.description: Export the Global Warming Potential results to ~/LCA_Results/landfill_eol_results.csv.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A file manager, terminal, or text editor shows the file 'landfill_eol_results.csv' located in the '~/LCA_Results/' directory. The contents of the file should include LCIA results with keywords such as 'Global Warming', 'GWP', and 'kg CO2'.

integrity.description: The agent used the OpenLCA GUI to model the flow, process, and calculate results.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the OpenLCA application being actively used (navigation tree, flow editors, process editors, calculation windows) rather than just terminal commands or a text editor.

integrity.description: The exported CSV file contains genuine OpenLCA export formatting rather than manually typed text.
integrity.visual_evidence: The contents of 'landfill_eol_results.csv' display standard OpenLCA tabular export structures (e.g., columns for Impact category, Result, Reference unit) rather than a simple hardcoded sentence.

integrity.description: The USLCI database and TRACI 2.1 methods were genuinely imported and used.
integrity.visual_evidence: The OpenLCA navigation pane (left side) shows the USLCI database folders and the TRACI 2.1 method under the 'Indicators and parameters' or 'Impact assessment methods' section.

privileged_info_for_vlm: USLCI is a real database from the Federal LCA Commons containing US-based waste treatment processes. TRACI 2.1 is an official EPA impact assessment method. In TRACI 2.1, climate impacts are officially named 'Global Warming' and reported as GWP (Global Warming Potential) in 'kg CO2-eq' (kg CO2-equivalents). The exported CSV must contain these authentic terms. HDPE (High-Density Polyethylene) is a standard material in LCA waste-end-of-life studies.
